faqs.question: What types of solutions does onsemi offer?
faqs.answer: onsemi offers comprehensive solutions for industrial, automotive, and renewable energy applications: Industrial Motor Drives - Complete variable frequency drive solutions using onsemi IGBTs and IPMs for pumps, fans, and machinery; Solar Inverters - High-efficiency photovoltaic inverter solutions with SiC and IGBT technology; EV Charging - Power solutions for electric vehicle charging infrastructure; Industrial Automation - Motor control and power conversion solutions for factory automation. Each solution includes optimized component selection, reference designs, and technical support from our FAE team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solutions page to find the right solution for your application, or contact our technical team for customized recommendations.

solutions.name: Industrial Motor Drive Solution
solutions.title: High-Efficiency Industrial Motor Drive with onsemi IGBTs and IPMs
solutions.description: Complete motor drive solution featuring onsemi IGBT modules and intelligent power modules for industrial AC motor control applications from 1kW to 75kW.
solutions.longDescription:
This comprehensive motor drive solution leverages onsemi's power semiconductor portfolio to deliver high-efficiency, reliable motor control for industrial applications. The solution features onsemi IGBT modules for the power stage, intelligent power modules for compact designs, and advanced gate drivers for optimal switching performance.

The design supports both V/Hz control and vector control algorithms, making it suitable for pumps, fans, compressors, conveyors, and general industrial machinery. Integrated protection features including overcurrent, overvoltage, and overtemperature ensure reliable operation in demanding industrial environments.
features: Intelligent Power Module option for compact designs

customerCases.challenge: A leading pump manufacturer needed to upgrade their fixed-speed pump systems to variable speed drives for energy savings. The challenge was to achieve >30% energy reduction while maintaining reliability in harsh industrial environments with high humidity and temperature variations.
customerCases.solution: Implemented onsemi-based motor drive solution using FGY75T120SWD IGBTs for 5.5kW pump motors. The solution included sensorless vector control for precise speed regulation, integrated protection features, and EMC-compliant design. The compact IP54-rated enclosure allowed outdoor installation.
results: 35% energy savings achieved compared to fixed-speed operation
Payback period of 18 months through energy cost reduction
Zero failures in 2 years of continuous operation
Reduced maintenance costs by 40% due to soft-start capability
customerCases.quote: The onsemi-based motor drive solution exceeded our expectations in both efficiency and reliability. The energy savings have significantly reduced our operating costs.
customerCases.contact: Contact us for detailed case study

customerCases.challenge: An HVAC integrator needed variable frequency drives for building automation systems. Requirements included silent operation for office environments, high efficiency for energy codes compliance, and compatibility with existing building management systems.
customerCases.solution: Deployed onsemi IGBT-based VFD solution with 16kHz switching frequency for silent operation. Used FGY100T120SWD for 15kW compressor drives and NCP51820 gate drivers. The solution featured RS485 Modbus communication for BMS integration and met ASHRAE 90.1 efficiency requirements.
results: Achieved <45dB noise level at 1 meter distance
42% energy reduction compared to traditional drives
Met all local energy code requirements
Seamless integration with existing BMS via Modbus
customerCases.quote: The onsemi solution delivered the perfect combination of efficiency, quiet operation, and system integration we needed for our building automation projects.
customerCases.contact: Contact us for detailed case study

author.name: David Chen
author.title: Senior FAE - Motor Control & Power Electronics

faeInsights.insight: After supporting hundreds of motor drive designs, I've found that the key to a successful industrial motor drive is balancing efficiency, reliability, and cost. The onsemi IGBT solution provides an excellent foundation with its robust trench-gate technology and competitive pricing. For drives in the 1-15kW range, the FGY series offers the optimal combination of performance and value. One critical design consideration is the gate drive circuit - using the recommended NCP51820 gate driver with proper layout ensures clean switching and minimizes EMI issues. I've also found that implementing proper thermal management from the start prevents field failures. Always use quality thermal interface material and ensure adequate heatsink sizing for worst-case conditions.
faeInsights.logic: The decision framework for motor drive design involves several key factors: First, determine your power requirements and select IGBTs with 30-50% current margin for reliability. Second, choose switching frequency based on your efficiency and audible noise requirements - 4-8kHz for general industrial, 16kHz+ for noise-sensitive applications. Third, implement comprehensive protection including desaturation detection, overcurrent shutdown, and thermal monitoring. Fourth, pay careful attention to PCB layout - minimize loop inductance in power paths and use Kelvin connections for gate drive. Finally, validate your design through thorough testing including thermal cycling, load steps, and fault condition verification.
keyTakeaways: Select IGBTs with 30-50% current margin for long-term reliability
Use recommended gate drivers with proper layout for clean switching
Implement comprehensive protection features from the start
Pay careful attention to thermal design and heatsink selection
Validate design through thorough testing including fault conditions
commonPitfalls: Insufficient gate drive current leading to slow switching and high losses
Inadequate thermal design causing premature device failure
Poor PCB layout resulting in excessive EMI and noise
Missing protection features leading to catastrophic failures
bestPractices: Use isolated gate drivers with desaturation protection
Implement soft-start to limit inrush current
Include DC bus precharge circuit for large capacitors
Design for worst-case thermal conditions with adequate margin
Use quality DC link capacitors with low ESR and ESL

faqs.question: What motor types are compatible with this motor drive solution?
faqs.answer: This motor drive solution supports various AC motor types: Induction Motors (IM) - Standard squirrel cage induction motors from 1kW to 75kW; Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM) - With appropriate control algorithm selection; Synchronous Reluctance Motors - For high-efficiency applications. The solution supports both V/Hz control for simple applications and sensorless vector control for high-performance requirements. For specialized motor types or specific application requirements, contact our FAE team for customization options.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to discuss your specific motor and application requirements for tailored solution recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I select the right IGBT rating for my motor drive application?
faqs.answer: IGBT selection for motor drives involves several considerations: Motor Power - Select IGBT current rating 1.5-2x motor rated current; Voltage Rating - Use 1200V IGBTs for 380-480VAC systems; Switching Frequency - Higher frequencies require IGBTs with lower switching losses; Overload Requirements - Consider motor starting current (typically 6-7x rated current); Thermal Design - Ensure adequate heatsink for continuous operation. For a 5.5kW motor (11A rated), FGY75T120SWD (75A) provides good margin. For 15kW motor (30A rated), FGY100T120SWD (100A) is recommended. Our FAE team can provide detailed selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our online selection tool or contact our FAE team for personalized IGBT selection based on your motor specifications.

faqs.question: What control algorithms are supported by this solution?
faqs.answer: The motor drive solution supports multiple control algorithms: V/Hz Control - Simple scalar control suitable for pumps, fans, and conveyors without high dynamic requirements; Sensorless Vector Control - High-performance control with torque and flux decoupling, suitable for applications requiring fast dynamic response; Slip Compensation - Maintains speed under load variations for V/Hz mode. The control algorithm can be selected based on application requirements. V/Hz is simpler and sufficient for many applications, while vector control provides superior performance for demanding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to discuss which control algorithm is best suited for your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in this motor drive solution?
faqs.answer: The motor drive solution includes comprehensive protection features: IGBT Protection - Desaturation detection, overcurrent shutdown, overvoltage clamping; Motor Protection - Overload protection, stall detection, phase loss detection; System Protection - Overvoltage/undervoltage on DC bus, overtemperature on heatsink and IGBTs, ground fault detection. All protection features are implemented with appropriate time delays to avoid nuisance trips while ensuring equipment safety. Fault diagnostics provide detailed information for troubleshooting.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review our detailed protection specifications or contact our technical team for specific protection requirements.

faqs.question: How can I integrate this motor drive with my existing control system?
faqs.answer: The motor drive solution offers multiple integration options: Analog Interface - 0-10V or 4-20mA for speed reference; Digital Interface - RS485 with Modbus RTU protocol for parameter setting and monitoring; Digital I/O - Run/stop, direction, fault output, speed reached indication; Communication Options - Optional CAN bus, Profibus, or Ethernet for industrial networks. The drive can be configured for local control via keypad, remote I/O control, or full integration with PLCs and SCADA systems. Configuration software allows easy parameter setup and monitoring.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to discuss your specific integration requirements and available communication options.

faqs.question: What is the typical payback period for upgrading to variable speed drives?
faqs.answer: Payback period for VSD upgrades depends on application and usage: Energy Savings - 20-40% typical for pumps and fans with variable load; 10-20% for constant torque applications. Payback Calculation - For a 15kW motor running 4000 hours/year with $0.10/kWh electricity cost and 30% energy savings: Annual savings = 15kW × 4000h × 0.30 × $0.10 = $1,800. With VSD cost of $2,500, payback period is approximately 17 months. Additional Benefits - Reduced mechanical stress extends motor and equipment life; Soft starting eliminates inrush current; Process optimization improves product quality. Many utilities offer rebates for VSD installations, further improving payback.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for a detailed ROI analysis based on your specific application and operating conditions.

solutions.name: Solar Inverter Solution
solutions.title: High-Efficiency Solar Inverter with onsemi SiC and IGBT Technology
solutions.description: Complete solar inverter solution featuring onsemi SiC MOSFETs and IGBTs for residential and commercial photovoltaic applications from 3kW to 50kW.
solutions.longDescription:
This comprehensive solar inverter solution leverages onsemi's advanced power semiconductor portfolio to deliver maximum energy harvest from photovoltaic systems. The solution features onsemi SiC MOSFETs for highest efficiency in the DC-DC boost stage and IGBT modules for the DC-AC inverter stage.

The design supports both single-phase and three-phase grid connection with power ratings from 3kW to 50kW. Advanced MPPT algorithms ensure optimal power extraction across varying irradiance conditions. Grid support functions including reactive power capability and anti-islanding protection meet modern grid code requirements.

customerCases.challenge: A solar EPC company needed high-efficiency inverters for a 500kW commercial rooftop project. Requirements included >98.5% efficiency, wide MPPT voltage range to accommodate different module configurations, and proven reliability for 25-year system lifetime.
customerCases.solution: Implemented onsemi-based 30kW three-phase inverters using NTHL040N65S3F MOSFETs for boost stage and FGY75T120SWD IGBTs for inverter. The solution achieved 98.7% maximum efficiency with wide 250-850V MPPT range. IP65 enclosures allowed outdoor installation without additional weather protection.
results: Additional 2.1% annual energy yield vs standard inverters
Zero failures in first 3 years of operation
Met all grid code requirements including reactive power support
customerCases.quote: The onsemi-based inverters delivered exceptional efficiency and reliability. The wide MPPT range accommodated our various string configurations perfectly.
customerCases.contact: Contact us for detailed case study

customerCases.challenge: A residential installer needed compact, quiet inverters for rooftop installations. Key requirements included silent operation, high efficiency for maximum ROI, and easy installation to reduce labor costs.
customerCases.solution: Deployed onsemi-based 5kW single-phase inverters with natural convection cooling for silent operation. Used NTHL025N65S3F for highest efficiency and FGY75T120SWD for inverter stage. Plug-and-play installation with integrated DC switch and WiFi monitoring.
results: Installation time reduced by 30% vs competitors
Homeowner app for monitoring increased customer satisfaction
customerCases.quote: Our customers love the silent operation and the monitoring app. The high efficiency means faster payback on their solar investment.
customerCases.contact: Contact us for detailed case study

author.name: Michael Zhang
author.title: Senior FAE - Renewable Energy & Power Conversion

faeInsights.insight: Having designed numerous solar inverters, I've learned that every 0.1% efficiency improvement directly translates to customer value over the 25-year system lifetime. The onsemi SiC and IGBT solution provides an excellent foundation for achieving >98.5% efficiency. For the boost stage, the NTHL series MOSFETs offer the optimal balance of low RDS(on) and switching performance. For the inverter stage, the FGY series IGBTs provide reliable operation at the 16-20kHz switching frequencies needed for low THD. One critical design consideration is the DC link capacitor selection - use high-quality film capacitors with low ESR for long lifetime. I've also found that proper thermal management is essential for outdoor installations where ambient temperatures can reach 60°C.
faeInsights.logic: The decision framework for solar inverter design focuses on maximizing efficiency and reliability: First, select power semiconductors with lowest total losses (conduction + switching) at your operating points. Second, optimize switching frequency - higher frequencies reduce magnetics size but increase switching losses; 16-20kHz is typical for good THD without excessive losses. Third, implement robust MPPT algorithm with fast tracking and minimal oscillation. Fourth, design for wide MPPT voltage range to accommodate various module configurations and temperature variations. Fifth, ensure comprehensive grid protection and anti-islanding per local codes. Finally, validate efficiency across the full operating range, not just at maximum power point.
keyTakeaways: Every 0.1% efficiency improvement provides significant customer value over system lifetime
Optimize switching frequency for balance between THD and switching losses
Use high-quality DC link capacitors with low ESR for long lifetime
Design for wide MPPT range to accommodate various module configurations
Implement comprehensive grid protection per local requirements
commonPitfalls: Undersizing magnetics leading to saturation and efficiency loss
Inadequate thermal design for high ambient temperature operation
Poor MPPT algorithm causing oscillation and reduced energy harvest
Insufficient EMI filtering causing grid connection issues
bestPractices: Use SiC or low-Qrr diodes for boost stage to minimize switching losses
Implement interleaved boost for high-power designs to reduce ripple
Design magnetics with adequate margin to prevent saturation
Include comprehensive monitoring for remote diagnostics
Validate efficiency across full operating voltage and power range

faqs.question: What is the efficiency advantage of using SiC MOSFETs in solar inverters?
faqs.answer: SiC MOSFETs provide significant efficiency advantages: Lower Switching Losses - Zero reverse recovery eliminates diode losses in boost stage; Higher Switching Frequency - Enables smaller magnetics and reduced losses; Lower Conduction Losses - Better RDS(on) vs silicon at high temperatures; Improved Efficiency - Typically 0.3-0.5% higher system efficiency vs silicon solutions. ROI Impact - For a 30kW system with 0.4% efficiency improvement, annual energy gain is ~420kWh. At $0.12/kWh, that's $50/year additional revenue. Over 25 years with 3% inflation, total value exceeds $1,800, justifying the SiC premium.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to evaluate SiC vs silicon solutions for your specific power level and efficiency targets.

faqs.question: How does the MPPT algorithm affect energy harvest?
faqs.answer: MPPT algorithm performance directly impacts energy harvest: Tracking Efficiency - Measures how accurately the algorithm finds the true maximum power point; Tracking Speed - Determines response time to changing irradiance conditions; Stability - Prevents oscillation around MPP that causes energy loss. Our solution achieves >99.5% MPPT efficiency with fast tracking and stable operation. In partial shading conditions, advanced algorithms can recover 10-30% more energy vs simple P&O methods. For systems with multiple MPPT inputs, independent tracking on each string prevents shading on one string from affecting others.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review our MPPT performance data or contact our technical team for detailed algorithm information.

faqs.question: What grid support functions are included in this solution?
faqs.answer: The solar inverter solution includes comprehensive grid support: Reactive Power - Adjustable power factor from 0.8 leading to 0.8 lagging; Voltage Ride-Through - Continues operation during grid voltage disturbances; Frequency Ride-Through - Supports grid stability during frequency variations; Anti-Islanding - Rapid detection and disconnection during grid outages; Power Curtailment - Remote or automatic power reduction when required; Ramp Rate Control - Gradual power changes to minimize grid impact. These functions ensure compliance with modern grid codes including VDE-AR-N 4105, IEEE 1547, and IEC 62116.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to confirm specific grid code compliance for your region.

faqs.question: How do I size a solar inverter for my installation?
faqs.answer: Solar inverter sizing considerations: DC/AC Ratio - Typically 1.1-1.3:1 for optimal performance; Oversizing - Inverter can be 10-20% smaller than array for cost savings; Clipping - Some energy loss during peak production is acceptable if overall economics improve; Climate - Hot climates may require larger inverters for same array size; Shading - Heavy shading may benefit from multiple smaller inverters. Example Sizing - 30kW array in moderate climate: 25kW inverter with 1.2:1 ratio. 50kW array with shading: Two 20kW inverters with independent MPPT. Our FAE team can provide detailed sizing analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for inverter sizing recommendations based on your specific array configuration and site conditions.

faqs.question: What monitoring and communication options are available?
faqs.answer: The solar inverter solution offers comprehensive monitoring: Local Display - LCD showing power, energy, voltage, current, and status; Remote Monitoring - Web portal and mobile app for real-time and historical data; Communication Interfaces - RS485 (Modbus), Ethernet, optional WiFi/GPRS; Data Logging - 25-year data storage with 5-minute resolution; Alerts - Email/SMS notifications for faults and performance issues; API Access - Third-party integration for fleet management. The monitoring system provides visibility into system performance, enabling proactive maintenance and maximizing uptime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team to discuss your specific monitoring and integration requirements.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of this solar inverter solution?
faqs.answer: The solar inverter solution is designed for 25-year system lifetime: Component Selection - High-quality components with appropriate derating; Thermal Design - Conservative thermal design ensures component temperatures stay well below ratings; Protection Features - Comprehensive protection prevents damage from faults; Cooling System - Robust cooling with redundancy where applicable; Serviceability - Modular design allows component replacement if needed. Warranty - 10-year standard warranty with extension options to 20-25 years. Expected Lifetime - Based on component stress analysis and accelerated life testing, >95% of units should operate 25 years with minimal maintenance. Key wear items (fans if equipped) may need replacement after 10-15 years.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for warranty details and extended warranty options.
